title: A fastener
abstract: The present invention provides a fastener (1) suitable for clamping a first component (3) to a second component (4) without the use of tools. The fastener (1) comprises a post (8) having a threaded proximal end (9) and a distal end (10) arranged to pass through aligned holes (7, 6) in the first and second components (3, 4) and to engage with a rear facing face of the second component (4), the fastener (1) comprising a nut (18) for screwing on the threaded proximal end of the post (8) and arranged to engage with a front facing face of the first component (3), such that screwing the nut (18) onto the post (8) may cause the first and second components (3, 4) to be clamped together. The fastener (1) has an integral ratchet mechanism (23, 24) acting between the post (8) and nut (18) which acts to prevents the nut (18) coming undone and which may permit hand tightening to be sufficient to prevent the fastener (1) from coming loose.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A fastener suitable for clamping a first component to a second component, the fastener comprising a post having a threaded proximal end and a distal end arranged to pass through aligned holes in the first and second components and to engage with a rear facing face of the second component, the fastener comprising a nut for screwing on the threaded proximal end of the post and arranged to engage with a front facing face of the first component, such that screwing the nut onto the post may cause the first and second components to be clamped together, the fastener being characterised in further comprising an integral ratchet mechanism 23, 24 acting between the post 8 and nut 18, which acts to prevent the nut rotating. 2. A fastener as claimed in Claim 1 wherein the ratchet mechanism defines a number of incremental rotational positions between the nut and the post and wherein the ratchet mechanism acts to retain the nut in one of the incremental positions, such that to release the fastening a predetermined minimum rotational force will need to be applied to cause the nut to rotate relative to the post from a first position to the next adjacent incremental position. 3. A fastener as claimed in Claim 2 comprising a spring biasing means arranged to urge the rotational position of the nut relative to the post to one of the incremental rotational positions. 4. A fastener as claimed in Claim 2 or 3 wherein the minimum rotational force is required to rotate the nut on the post at all times, including when the fastening has been released. 5. A fastener as claimed in any preceding claim further comprising a plate located on the post between the threaded proximal end and the distal end and arranged to lie in a plane substantially orthogonal to a longitudinal axis of the post, the ratchet plate being arranged to move in a longitudinal direction along at least a portion of the post, the ratchet plate comprising a first engagement portion 

 arranged to engage with a second engagement portion, the second engagement portion being on, or fixed to, the nut, with one or both of the first and second engagement portions forming a ratchet to engage with said other engagement portion, the post and ratchet plate being shaped or provided with a keyway or spline, to prevent rotation between the post and the ratchet plate; and a spring acting between the ratchet plate and the post or the nut in order to urge the first and second engagement portions together. 6. A fastener as claimed in Claim 5 wherein the action of tightening the nut on the post compresses the spring and increases the pressure on the first and second engagement portions and wherein tightening the nut also causes the first and second engagement portions to rotate relative to each other, with the ratchet forcing the ratchet plate away from the second engagement portion against the action of the spring in order for rotation to occur. 7. A fastener as claimed in Claim 5 or 6 wherein the second engagement portion is on, or fixed to, the nut and wherein the post has an outwardly extending portion engaging with a first end of the spring, to compress the spring such that a second end of the spring forces the ratchet plate into contact with the second engagement portion. 8. A fastener as claimed in Claim 7 wherein the nut is in the form of an internally threaded cap and the second engagement portion comprises an annular distal facing serrated portion of the cap. 9. A fastener as claimed in any one of Claims 1 to 4 wherein at least one of an inner surface of the nut, or an outer surface of the post has a ratchet shaped profile formed on it, or attached to it, the fastener further comprising a spring biasing means to urge a component fixed relative to the other of the nut or the post into contact with the ratchet surface. 10. A fastener as claimed in Claim 9 wherein the nut has the ratchet shaped profile formed an in inward facing face that engages with an outwardly biased component rotationally fixed to the post. 1 1. A fastener as claimed in Claim 9 or 10 wherein the component retains contact with the ratchet shaped profile as the nut is unscrewed from the post. 12. A fastener as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the distal end of the post comprises multiple axially extending fingers each having a barb at a distal end for engaging with a rear face of the second component, the post having a aperture extending along a central longitudinal axis to define a longitudinally extending lumen, the fastener further comprising a pin shaped to pass down the lumen to the fingers and urge the fingers radially outwards, such that the pin causes the fingers to extend outwardly until the barbs extend to a circumference greater than a main portion of the post, so that they may extend under a rear face of the second component. 13. A fastener as claimed in Claim 12 wherein one of an outer surface of the pin or an inner surface of the lumen has an annular groove arranged to cooperate with an annular ridge, or protrusions, on the other of the inner surface of the lumen or outer surface of the pin, to lock the pin in a depressed position where the fingers are forced radially outwards. 14. A fastener as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 1 1 wherein the distal end of the post comprises a claw pivotally attached to the distal end of the post, which claw may adopt a retracted position where it does not substantially extend radially beyond the circumference of the remainder of the distal end of the post, and an extended position where it does substantially extend radially beyond the circumference of the remainder of the distal end of the post, so that in the extended position the claw may extend under a rear face of the second component, the post having an aperture extending along a central longitudinal axis to define a 

 longitudinally extending lumen, the fastener further comprising a pin or wire passing through the lumen arranged to deploy or retract the claw. 15. A fastener as claimed in Claim 14 comprising a pin passing through the lumen and attached at a distal end to the claw, wherein depressing the pin causes the claw to adopt the extended position and wherein retracting the pin causes the claw to adopt the retracted position, permitting the fastener to be removed. 16. A fastener as claimed in any one of claims 12, 13 or 15 wherein, in the depressed position, the pin is recessed within the nut and wherein the fastener further comprises a removable key arranged to engage in a proximal end of the pin and extract the pin to permit the fastener to be removed. 17. A fastener as claimed in any proceeding claim wherein the ratchet is a two way ratchet, working both when the nut is tightened and when the nut is released, but wherein the ratchet is dimensioned such that if the nut is torqued to a value to tighten it, then a torque of a greater value is required to release it. 18. A fastener as claimed in any proceeding claim wherein the nut is arranged to be turned by hand and wherein the dimensions and properties of the fastener are selected such that the first and second components may be clamped with a desired compressive force by tightening the nut by hand and wherein a tool will normally be subsequently required to undo the nut against the force exerted by the ratchet. 19. A fastener as claimed in any proceeding claim arranged to clamp first and second components together having a known combined thickness between said front and rear facing faces, wherein the nut is in the form of an open ended internally threaded cap and wherein, when the fastener is correctly tightened, a tip of the proximal end of the post is substantially flush with a top surface of the cap to provide a visual indication that the fastener is correctly fastened. 20. A fastener as claimed in any proceeding claim wherein the post has an aperture extending along a central longitudinal axis to define a longitudinally extending lumen, the fastener further comprising a pin arranged to be depressed to enable the distal end of the post to engage with a rear facing face of the second component, wherein the fastener is arranged and intended to be correctly fitted and fastened using only a single hand, without the use of tools, to push the fitting into an appropriate pair of in-register holes extending through first and second components to be clamped together, while simultaneously depressing the pin and subsequently tightening the nut. 21. A method of fastening a DIN rail or terminal block to a frame, the method comprising aligning a preformed hole in the DIN rail or terminal block with a hole in the frame, inserting a fastener as recited in any preceding claim and fastening the fastener in place without the use of tools. 22. A method of fastening a DIN rail or terminal block to a metal frame of an aircraft, the method comprising aligning a preformed hole in the DIN rail or terminal block with a hole in the frame, inserting a fastener as recited in any preceding claim and fastening the fastener in place, without the use of tools.
